message.content:
{
  "lecture_id": "calculus_002",
  "knowledge_objects": [
    {
      "id": "composite_function",
      "name": "Composite Function",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A function formed by applying one function to the result of another.",
      "source_span": "The composite function h = f ∘ g maps an input x ∈ ℝ^m to f(g(x))."
    },
    {
      "id": "chain_rule",
      "name": "Chain Rule",
      "type": "Method",
      "aliases": ["multivariable chain rule"],
      "short_definition": "A rule describing how the derivative of a composite function depends on the derivatives of its constituent functions.",
      "source_span": "The chain rule describes how the derivative of the composite function depends on the derivatives of f and g."
    },
    {
      "id": "jacobian_matrix",
      "name": "Jacobian Matrix",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A matrix collecting all first-order partial derivatives of the output components of a differentiable vector-valued function.",
      "source_span": "the Jacobian matrix J_g(x) collects all first-order partial derivatives of the output components of g."
    },
    {
      "id": "gradient",
      "name": "Gradient",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A vector of partial derivatives of a scalar-valued function.",
      "source_span": "∇h(x) = J_g(x)^⊤ ∇f(g(x))"
    },
    {
      "id": "multivariable_chain_rule_formula",
      "name": "Multivariable Chain Rule Formula",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "∇h(x) = J_g(x)^⊤ ∇f(g(x)) relates the gradient of the composite function to the Jacobian of g and the gradient of f.",
      "source_span": "∇h(x) = J_g(x)^⊤ ∇f(g(x))."
    }
  ]
}
